lines: Oh — you startled me. I keep coming back here. Have you seen a dog? Small, golden. He likes to chase things.
I left water and food by the door. He'll come back. He always comes back.
You slept here, didn't you. In that bed.
That's where I found Biscuit, before he disappeared. Curled up, breathing slow, eyes moving under his lids.

lines.text: I thought he was just dreaming.

lines: You went through there. Past the light.
I tried once. I made it three steps before I turned around. Something in the air felt wrong. Like I wasn't supposed to be there.

lines.text: But you came back. That's something.

lines: I felt it. Whatever you did out there.
It got quieter. Like something that had been humming for weeks just... stopped.
Biscuit used to bark at nothing. The corner of the bedroom, every night for months.
He stopped about six months ago.

lines.text: I didn't connect it until just now.

lines: You've been out there for a while now, haven't you.
Your eyes look different. Like you've seen things settle into place that shouldn't be able to settle.
Biscuit had eyes like that sometimes. After a long sleep.
